If $n$ is a positive integer larger than 1, let $n = \prod p_i^{k_i}$ be the
unique prime factorization of $n$, where the $p_i$'s are distinct primes, 2, 3,
5, 7, 11, $\dots$, and define $f(n)$ by $f(n) = \sum k_i p_i$ and $g(n)$ by
$\displaystyle{g(n) = \lim_{m \to \infty} f^m(n)}$, where $f^m$ is meant the
$m$-fold application of $f$. Then $n$ is said to have property $H$ if $n/2 <
g(n) < n$.

\begin{enumerate}[(a)]
\item Evaluate $g(100)$ and $g(10^{10})$.
\item Find all positive odd integers larger than 1 that have property $H$.
\end{enumerate}
